steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure around the blank
steps.content: The blank falls at the end of an introductory dependent clause. What follows is an independent clause. The rule for this structure is clear: place a comma after the dependent clause to separate it from the independent clause that follows. The blank must supply that comma after the word lifelike.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "lifelike but": Adding "but" after the dependent clause creates a conjunction that mangles the sentence structure; "but" is not needed here. ✗
  • "lifelike": No comma means no separation between the introductory dependent clause and the independent clause, violating standard punctuation. ✗
  • "lifelike,": The comma correctly separates the introductory dependent clause from the independent clause that follows. ✓
  • "lifelike, but": The comma is correct, but adding "but" introduces a redundant conjunction that creates a grammatical error. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
